name: Papiol, Ermita de la Salut, Can Barba – Puig Madrona – Circular Route
name_original: Papiol, Ermita de la Salut, Can Barba – Puig Madrona – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around El Papiol offers diverse landscapes within the Parc Natural de Collserola, known as Barcelona's "green lung." The region features a Mediterranean climate and varied terrain, supporting mixed woodlands of white pines and evergreen oaks, alongside riverside copses and farmland. Hikers can discover natural springs and enjoy panoramic views of Barcelona and the Vallès region. This ecological diversity provides a habitat for various wildlife. Best hiking trails around El Papiol The most popular hiking route is Papiol, Ermita de la Salut, Can Barba – Puig Madrona – Circular Route, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes landmarks like Ermita de la Salut and Puig Madrona. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hermitage of Our Lady of Health – Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall loop from el Papiol, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.0 km) path. This trail features the Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall and the Hermitage of Our Lady of Health. Local hikers also love the Puig Madrona Summit – Hermitage of Our Lady of Health loop from el Papiol, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through woodlands and past the Puig Madrona Summit, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around El Papiol is defined by mixed woodlands, natural springs, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate trails. The routes in El Papiol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 670 reviews. More than 3,500 hikers have used komoot to explore El Papiol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Papiol?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;13 distinct hiking routes&lt;/strong&gt; around El Papiol, offering a variety of experiences within the Parc Natural de Collserola. These trails range from easy strolls to more challenging moderate hikes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in El Papiol?
A: Yes, El Papiol offers &lt;strong&gt;2 easy hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These routes provide a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in El Papiol?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in El Papiol are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938984183/papiol-ermita-de-la-salut-can-barba-puig-madrona-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papiol, Ermita de la Salut, Can Barba – Puig Madrona – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.3-mile (8.5 km) loop.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can discover the beautiful &lt;strong&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/strong&gt; on one of the moderate hiking loops. This natural feature is a highlight for many hikers exploring the area. Additionally, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2765506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Grotto in Parc de Torreblanca&lt;/a&gt; offers another scenic spot.

Q: What kind of natural features and wildlife can I expect to see?
A: The trails in El Papiol traverse the diverse landscapes of the Parc Natural de Collserola, featuring &lt;strong&gt;mixed woodlands of white pines and evergreen oaks&lt;/strong&gt;, as well as riverside copses. You might encounter natural springs and a variety of wildlife, including squirrels, foxes, and wild boars, as the park is home to approximately 190 species of vertebrates.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, several historical and cultural attractions are integrated into the hiking routes. You can visit the &lt;strong&gt;Ermita de la Salut (Hermitage of Our Lady of Health)&lt;/strong&gt;, a significant landmark featured in multiple itineraries. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33830222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Health – Puig Madrona Observation Tower loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that includes both the hermitage and the observation tower.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Papiol?
A: The hiking trails in El Papiol are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.3 stars from over 670 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views of Barcelona and the Vallès region, and the well-maintained paths within the natural park.

Q: Are the trails in El Papiol suitable for families with children?
A: While there are 11 moderate trails, El Papiol also offers &lt;strong&gt;2 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; that are generally suitable for families. These shorter, less strenuous options allow families to enjoy the natural surroundings without tackling challenging terrain. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in El Papiol?
A: The Parc Natural de Collserola, which encompasses El Papiol, generally allows dogs on trails, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Check local park regulations for any specific restrictions or designated off-leash areas.

Q: What are the best viewpoints to experience panoramic views?
A: Many trails in El Papiol offer spectacular views. The &lt;strong&gt;Puig Madrona Observation Tower&lt;/strong&gt; is a key point of interest providing scenic vistas. Additionally, the famous 'Carretera de les Aigües' within the Collserola Natural Park offers stunning panoramic views of both Barcelona and the Vallès region.

Q: Are there any caves or gorges to explore nearby?
A: Yes, the region around El Papiol features interesting geological formations. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Juanita Mine&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt;. For gorges, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1423555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2956234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Escletxes del Papiol&lt;/a&gt; for unique natural experiences.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in El Papiol?
A: Hiking durations in El Papiol vary significantly. Shorter, easy walks can be completed in under two hours, while some of the moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14913283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Madrona Summit – Hermitage of Our Lady of Health loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 3 hours 10 minutes. More extensive routes can exceed five hours, catering to different fitness levels and time commitments.
tips: A hermitage from the 9th century, renovated in the 11th century, is really beautiful, in a cozy setting within a neat and quiet space, ideal to go up to see it during the week and rest there for a while.

Here you can see a cagatió, a Christmas character from Catalan culture. The cagatió is a wooden log, usually with wooden legs, a cap, and a smiling face. On Christmas Eve, the cagatió defecates gifts for children. The tió originally has rural, pre-Christian origins.

The way to the source is indicated by signs. La Font de Can Barba is at one end of a small square with good shade in summer. The entrance is formed by an arch that gives access to a small enclosure where the fountain is. If the year has been good in rains, the source has abundant water.

Also known as Can Barba de Madrona, it is located at the foot of Puig Madrona, on the path that leads to Can Domènech. The first documents that tell us about the existence of the farmhouse date back to the 13th century. During the French War (1808-1812), the farmhouse was looted and destroyed by Napoleon's troops. During the Spanish Civil War (1936-1939) the farmhouse was seized and became a weapons depot and powder keg. It currently belongs to the Companyia de Jesús.

Currently there is an equestrian center but the building on the property was built at the end of the s. XV or beginning of the s. XVI and its interior preserves remains of the time. The property has a sundial that has a frame in which the date (1868) is inscribed and a phrase: "The hours flee - quickly they flee - and they do not return."
